claims.claim: Speculative decoding in batched LLM serving can reduce latency but introduces wasted draft computation and KV-cache pressure when acceptance rates are low.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: We present a speculation-cost governor that adaptively caps per-request speculative-token limits, controls admission, and applies bounded dwell delays under KV pressure, integrated into vLLM's V1 scheduler via an OpenAI-compatible control contract (`vllm_xargs`).
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: In synthetic concurrent serving benchmarks, the governor reduced wasted draft work by 2.5–4.7% and peak KV usage by 0.7–5.2% at the cost of 2.1–15.3% p95 latency regression and 5.8–11.2% throughput loss.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: In real patched-vLLM benchmarks on an NVIDIA GB10 system with SmolLM2-135M and ngram speculation, the governor achieved a 29.5% p95 latency reduction and 28.5% throughput improvement under KV/concurrency stress, and negligible overhead (+0.94% p95, −1.7% throughput) under dwell stress.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Results are bounded to the tested hardware, model, and concurrency levels; the governor's benefit is contingent on KV-pressure conditions and speculative acceptance rates.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: In batched serving settings (e.g., vLLM, SGLang), speculative decoding interacts with KV-cache management, request scheduling, and admission control in ways that can degrade rather than improve throughput when acceptance rates are low or KV pressure is high.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: This work investigates whether a lightweight, per-request speculation-cost governor—adaptively capping speculative-token limits, applying bounded dwell delays under KV pressure, and controlling admission—can reduce wasted draft work and KV pressure in batched vLLM serving without exceeding a 20% p95 latency regression threshold.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: We evaluate the governor across three tiers of evidence: (1) in-process synthetic benchmarks, (2) HTTP endpoint benchmarks against a local OpenAI-compatible shim, and (3) real patched-vLLM benchmarks with a CUDA-backed model server.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: The speculation-cost governor (`src/batched_spec_governor.py`) operates as a serving control-plane component that makes per-request decisions along three axes: 1.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: Speculative-token cap.** The governor adaptively limits the number of draft tokens a request may propose, based on current KV pressure, recent acceptance rates (tracked via EWMA feedback), and request-level features (prompt length, expected output length).
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: A patch installer (`scripts/install_vllm_spec_governor_patch.py`) and unified diff (`patches/vllm_spec_governor_contract.patch`) modify three vLLM V1 source files: - **`vllm/v1/request.py`:** Parses `vllm_xargs.spec_governor` from `SamplingParams.extra_args` into a `Request.spec_governor` attribute.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: `vllm/v1/core/sched/scheduler.py`:** Applies per-request speculative-token caps to draft token scheduling lookahead and per-request KV allocation.
claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.
